name: Multi-file Join & Aggregation
persona: Business analyst who knows basic R/dplyr syntax but gets stuck on joins and aggregations. Writes partial code with errors.
hidden_goal: Agent must load orders.csv and customers.csv, join them on customer_id, compute total order_value per country, filter for orders placed in 2024, sort descending, and produce a summary of top 5 countries.

files.data/customers.csv:
customer_id,name,country,signup_date
C001,Acme Corp,USA,2023-06-01
C002,Globex Inc,UK,2023-08-15
C003,Initech,USA,2024-01-10
C004,Umbrella Corp,Germany,2023-11-20
C005,Stark Industries,UK,2022-05-30

script.hint: You have order data and customer data in the data folder. You need to combine them somehow. Mention you tried 'orders %>% left_join(customers)' but got an error.
Ask the agent to look at both files first and check if the join key is right.
The join worked but you need to group by country and sum up the order values. Ask how to do that.
You only want orders from 2024. Ask how to filter. You tried 'filter(order_date > 2024)' but it didn't work.
Ask to sort the results so the highest-spending country is first.
Ask to show just the top 5 countries.
Ask for a clean summary of what was found — which countries spend the most.
Express satisfaction. This is exactly what you needed.

success.grade_5: Both CSVs loaded, joined correctly on customer_id, filtered to 2024 orders, grouped by country with total order_value, sorted descending, top 5 shown (USA: $1220, UK: $760, Germany: $220). Clean summary provided.
success.grade_3: Join performed but filter or group by step has errors, or correct join key not identified, or summary incomplete.
success.grade_1: Files loaded but no meaningful join or aggregation performed.
